builds.tips: Aino's A1 talent allows her Elemental Burst to attack more frequently and have a larger AoE DMG if Moonsign: Ascendant Gleam is active. This requires 2 or more Nod-Krai characters in the party. Aino herself counts towards this.

notes:
OFF-FIELD SUPPORT
Aino can offer Off-Field Hydro application with her Elemental Burst. She does not possess a Moon Wheel, and cannot contribute to Lunar-Reactions as the sole member in the team from Nod-Krai. Furthermore, Aino heavily favours having another member from Nod-Krai on the team (for a total of 2 or more.) See the ability tips for more details. Aino's scalings are pitifully low, thus, her damage investment is not a priority.

Aino's [C4] gives her 10 Energy upon landing her Elemental Skill on an enemy, effectively changing her burst cost from 50 to 40. This has a cooldown of 10 seconds.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Flame-Forged Insight: If Aino cannot trigger the specified Elemental Reactions, this weapon is not worth using. This weapon does not need to be leveled if you do not care about Aino's personal damage.
Favonius Greatsword: With this weapon, you want to build some Crit Rate to help proc the passive.
Makhaira Aquamarine: If the team does not benefit from the ATK% buff, this weapon is not worth using.
Master Key: Refinements for this weapon are not needed if you do not care about Aino's personal damage.

Regarding Artifact Choices:
Aino's artifact choices are very interchangeable depending on the team that she is in and what that team desires. You can choose sets based on your team's energy needs, buffing needs or based on what member holds what artifact set from the list.

notes:
OFF-FIELD DPS
Albedo is a character that can summon a Geo Construct from his Elemental Skill which deals damage on a 2 second interval whenever enemies in the field are damaged. His Elemental Burst provides 125 EM to the party, which is good on teams that rely on amplification reactions, such as Vaporize. It is worth noting that since his stat priority is focused on DEF (Elemental Skill scaling), you might want to skip casting Elemental Burst if your team doesn't benefit from the 125 EM buff.

OFF-FIELD DPS
Regarding Weapon Choices:
Peak Patrol Song: Make sure you Normal Attack twice before or after using his Skill with this weapon.
Harbinger of Dawn: Assumes the passive is active. Without the passive active most of the time, this weapon is not worth using.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Husk of Opulent Dreams (4): Due to Albedo's skill snapshotting upon cast, you need to either wait ~12 seconds before starting a domain/fight to reach max stacks or recast his skill to benefit from the DEF and Geo DMG bonus provided by the 4 piece set effect.
Tenacity of the Millelith (4): This set is used for the 4 piece set effect which has good uptime on Albedo. Worth noting that this set decreases your Elemental Skill damage by a lot if compared to the other offensive sets.
Archaic Petra (4): This set is a niche option for Albedo. It is lower personal damage than several other set options, but can be a reasonable option in some teams for buffing other characters. Do note that the 4pc bonus requires you to pick the shard up on Albedo. 

Regarding Artifact Main Stats:
Goblet: DEF% is generally preferred for team damage unless Albedo already has, or is close to 3,000 DEF in combat.

builds.tips:
Basic Rotations:

Burst up = Q > E > N2D + 2N3D + N1C + 2N2C

Burst down = E > N1C + 5N2C + N1

notes:
SPREAD DPS
This playstyle maximizes Alhaitham's kit, leveraging both Spread reactions and his Elemental Mastery-scaling skill for significant damage. As a Spread DPS with a high rate of on-field Dendro application and flexible team compositions, he excels in Quicken/Quickbloom teams. To further maximise his performance it's highly recommended to prioritize levelling him to 90/90.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Light of Foliar Incision: This weapon prefers EM sands even in situations with fewer or no Spread procs such as in Bloom/Burgeon/Hyperbloom teams.
Toukabou Shigure: This ranking assumes single-target situations, in AoE situations it starts falling behind weapons such as Iron Sting and Blackcliff Longsword.
Harbinger of Dawn: If you can maintain near perfect uptime on its passive this weapon will perform similarly to weapons like Haran Geppaku Futsu.
Xiphos' Moonlight / Festering Desire: These weapons can perform similarly to The Black Sword in teams where you have higher Energy Recharge requirements or want to burst every rotation.
Wolf-Fang: At [R5], this weapon performs similarly to Haran Geppaku Futsu and Mistsplitter Reforged.
Uraku Misugiri: With a Geo unit on the team this performs on par with Light of Foliar Incision, or even slightly better in AoE scenarios where you lose stacks faster on Foliar.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Gilded Dreams (4): This ranking assumes that you already have another Deepwood Memories holder on the team, if not then Deepwood Memories becomes his best set.
Deepwood Memories (2) / Emblem of Severed Fate (2) / +80 EM set (2): These split set options can actually perform similarly to Gilded Dreams (4) or even surpass it in situations where you have higher Energy Recharge requirements or want to burst every rotation. Or even if they just have better substat rolls on them.
Marechaussee Hunter (4): If played with Furina, this is Alhaitham's best set.

Regarding Main Stat Priorities: Usually in Quicken/Quickbloom teams Alhaitham leans towards Elemental Mastery sands (roughly 5% or so difference usually). But in Bloom/Burgeon/Hyperbloom teams with, fewer to no Spread reactions, he skews more towards ATK% sands (unless you're using Light of Foliar Incision). Or even Energy Recharge sands if you're trying to burst every rotation for increased Dendro seed generation in those teams.

Detailed weapon comparisons here.

2. ATK%

4. Energy Recharge

notes:
BURST SUPPORT
Aloy is much preferred as a Burst Support over Main DPS as getting coil stacks for her Cryo infusion can be quite unreliable. Aloy needs 4 coil stacks to get the Cryo infusion and you can only get 1 stack every 0.1 seconds which is quite hard to do considering the Bomblets are scattered all over the place. One thing to note regarding Aloy's coil stacks is that she can still get coil stacks even when she's off-field.

Aloy is very niche as she only performs well in certain comps and even then, she's still completely replaceable by other characters. She can be utilized on a Freeze team where she can be a battery and nuke at the same time since she provides 5 Cryo particles on her Elemental Skill. She can also be utilized on a Melt team where you can melt her Elemental Burst for quite a decent amount of damage.

Regarding Weapon Choice:
Polar Star: Forcing to get Polar Star stacks isn't worth due to consuming extra time to get those stacks.
The Stringless: Placement assumes Aloy is used on a Reverse Melt team. On Reverse Melt Aloy Stringless, especially at higher refinement levels, can pull ahead of Thundering Pulse.
Prototype Crescent: Assumes the passive is active by proccing a weak point shot. This weapon especially at higher refinement levels can be as good as Skyward Harp.
Sacrificial Bow: A pretty decent option to make Aloy a Cryo battery as her Elemental Skill provides 5 Cryo particles.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Blizzard Strayer (4): This artifact set is best used on a Freeze-focused Aloy as it provides a ton of Crit Value.
+80 EM set (2): This artifact set bonus only benefits a Melt-focused Aloy, Elemental Mastery provides nothing on a Freeze Aloy.
Noblesse Oblige (4): Aloy is one of the best Noblesse Oblige (4) holders since she has low energy requirements and cooldown for her Elemental Burst.

Regarding Artifact Main Stat and Substats Priority: 
Elemental Mastery is only an option if your Aloy is played on a Reverse Melt team as it provides nothing to a Freeze Aloy.

builds.main_stats:
Sands - DEF%
Goblet - Geo DMG
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge*
2. Crit Rate / DMG
3. DEF%
4. ATK%

*Prioritize Energy Recharge until you meet the requirement.

notes:
DPS
Arataki Itto is a Geo DPS Character who takes advantage of his Elemental Burst's ATK boost and Geo infusion to deal significant damage during its uptime. His combos involve stacking and unleashing his Charged Attacks, named Arataki Kesagiri. Arataki Kesagiri consumes no stamina and can be cast continuously as long as he has Superlative Superstrength stacks. Superlative Superstrength is capped at 5 stacks at any given time, and Itto gets those stacks when his 2nd and 4th Normal Attack string hits an enemy. Activating his Elemental Burst allows his 1st and 3rd Normal Attack to also generate stacks. Itto's Elemental Skill deals damage to enemies on cast and summons a taunt, Ushi, which is considered a Geo Construct. This initial cast damage grants Itto 1 Superlative Superstrength stack, and upon Ushi leaving the field, Itto is granted another stack. When Ushi takes damage, Itto gains a stack (capped at 1 stack per 2 seconds). Itto's Elemental Burst converts his Normal and Charged Attacks into Geo DMG, increases Itto's Normal Attack Speed and converts a portion of Itto's current DEF to ATK.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Serpent Spine: This weapon scales great with refines on Itto,  making it way better than similar ranking options at [R5]. When using Husk of Opulent Dreams, Serpent Spine stacks are prioritized over Husk of Opulent Dreams stacks due to their conflicting nature in gaining stacks (On-Field vs Off-Field).
Skyward Pride: Can be a great or bad weapon for Itto depending on your ER needs. The less Energy Recharge you need (due to particle generation from your party members or ER from subs), the less useful this weapon becomes.
Whiteblind: Your rotations often start with 1 stack of this weapon, and due to Itto's Burst snapshotting DEF on cast, you're unable to take advantage of further DEF% from the stacks.

Regarding Artifact Sets: 
Retracing Bolide (4): This set grants you an additional 40% Normal and Charged Attack DMG when shielded. Crystallize can be unreliable so a consistent shielder is preferred, such as Zhongli.

Detailed weapon comparisons here.

Arlecchino is a Pyro DPS that focuses on dealing damage through her Bond of Life mechanic. It should be noted that while in combat, Arlecchino cannot be healed by any source other than her Elemental Burst. For this reason, a shielder is strongly recommended within her teams.

Regarding Weapon Choice:
Weapon ranking is assuming Arlecchino is used with Bennett.

Desert Pavilion Chronicle (4): Don't cancel Arlecchino's Charged Attack too early to ensure that it hits opponents for the passive effect.
Echoes of an Offering (4): This set performs similarly to Desert Pavilion Chronicle (4) if your ping is consistently lower than 200.

Arlecchino's Elemental Skill and Burst do not need as high of an investment as her Normal Attack talent does.

Sands - HP% / Energy Recharge
Goblet - HP%
Circlet - HP% / Healing Bonus*

*Check notes
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge*
2. HP%
3. Flat HP
4. Crit Rate*

*Prioritize Energy Recharge until requirement is met. Crit Rate is only recommended when using Favonius Codex.
builds.tips: Baizhu's Elemental Skill has a 2-3 second delay between cast time and heal received, keep this in mind when swapping to low HP allies after Baizhu's rotation.

notes:
SUPPORT
Baizhu provides teamwide healing through the use of his Elemental Skill, and heals the active character from his Burst. The shields generated from his Burst should be considered as interrupt resistance as opposed to a reliable form of damage mitigation for non-Bloom reaction related damage sources. Due to being a Dendro catalyst, Baizhu can work effectively in a large range of different Dendro team archetypes. 

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Hakushin Ring: This weapon is best used in Quicken teams when Baizhu is able to provide reasonable uptime on the buff. 
Prototype Amber: In addition to the amount of flat energy generation for the user, this weapon can provide significant team-wide healing when used in conjunction with Healing Bonus. It is a great option when using Baizhu with Furina for building Fanfare points from the second rotation onwards.
Favonius Codex: Try to build some Crit Rate when using this weapon in order to trigger the passive effect more consistently. 
A Thousand Floating Dreams: This weapon can be considered in certain teams for the modest Elemental Mastery team buff it provides, though there are generally better uses for it. 

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Deepwood Memories (4): Under AoE situations, it may be better for another party member to hold this set as Baizhu has lackluster AoE.
Instructor (4): The Elemental Mastery gained from this set outweighs the reduction in A4 passive bonus from lower main stat values.
Noblesse Oblige (4): In teams that benefit from ATK%, this set can be run over Instructor (4) if the additional sustain from higher HP values is preferred.

Regarding Artifact Stats: 
While generally in teams that can benefit from Baizhu's Ascension 4 passive you want to build high HP (up to 50k) to maximize the buff it can provide, Healing Bonus circlet can be considered over HP% in teams where having more healing would be valuable. For example, using Healing Bonus circlet can allow for building Fanfare points faster when using Baizhu with Furina, moreover Healing Bonus circlet synergizes well with Prototype Amber's passive.

notes:
SUPPORT
Bennett's main utility as a Support comes from the huge flat ATK buff his Elemental Burst provides. This buff only scales with his Base Attack, so it is unaffected by any external ATK Buff.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Mistsplitter Reforged, Azurelight and Absolution: While providing the same base ATK as Aquila Favonia, These weapons are a better choice when building for Bennett's personal damage .
Freedom-Sworn: In teams where Bennett can reliably trigger this weapon's passive, it can be stronger than Aquila Favonia, Mistsplitter Reforged and Absolution.
Peak Patrol Song: While requiring two additional normal attacks, this weapon can provide a sizeable buff with just one or two DEF% Main Stats, making it competitive with Aquila Favonia and similar options for teams that highly value DMG% buffs.
Favonius Sword: This weapon's low base attack heavily impairs Bennett's buffing potency, but it can be a viable pick in teams with extremely high Energy recharge requirements or no actual use for the attack buff.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Instructor (4): In teams that value Elemental Mastery more than ATK%, this set pulls ahead of other options if Bennett can reliably trigger a reaction.
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4): This set requires Bennett to trigger a reaction including the Element you want to be buffed. When Noblesse is already covered on the team, or the team values DMG Bonus more than Attack or Elemental Mastery, this set is a good pick.
Deepwood Memories (4): The best pick for any Dendro Damage related teams Bennett might be in. Use this if there is no other viable holder available.

DPS
Regarding Weapon Choices:
Haran Geppaku Futsu, Light of Foliar Incision, The Black Sword: When Bennett is not able to use infused Normal Attacks, these weapons perform significantly worse.
Freedom-Sworn, The Alley Flash: If Bennett is not able to trigger Melt or Vaporize frequently, these weapons perform poorly.
Summit Shaper: Assumes no shield. With a shield, this weapon performs better than similar ranking weapons.
Wolf-Fang: At [R5], this weapon performs significantly better, matching Summit Shaper.
Finale of the Deep: If you can reliably clear the Bond of Life, this weapon performs similar to Summit Shaper.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Crimson Witch of Flames (4): This set is the best generalist pick for Bennett, as it performs great when triggering Vaporize or Melt, and still good when not triggering reactions.
Thundering Fury (4): In teams where Bennett frequently triggers electro related reactions, such as overload, this set can perform on par with Crimson Witch of Flames (4).
Marechaussee Hunter (4): When paired with Furina, this is Bennett's best set.

Circlet - HP% / Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge 
2. Crit Rate* 
3. HP%
4. Flat HP 

*Crit Rate only if running Favonius Lance
builds.tips: In some teams, Candace's hold Elemental Skill cooldown is too long for rotations to work properly, so if Candace isn't C4, it's recommended to avoid getting hit and to do a tap Skill as quick as possible.

notes:
BUFF SUPPORT
Candace is a 4* Hydro Support that specializes in providing off-field Hydro application with her HP% scaling Burst, infusing the autos of melee allies (Sword/Claymore/Polearm) with Hydro via her Burst's Prayer of the Crimson Crown effect, and providing defensive utility with her Elemental Skill. It is worth noting that Candace will never be the strongest option for your teams; at the cost of having different kinds of utility options in one kit, she will never be the best at them. Her damage contribution will be surpassed by other offensive-type Hydro supports like Xingqiu and her defensive capabilities are outclassed by the healing capabilities of the other Hydro healers. Thus, it is recommended to focus on her strength as a consistent Hydro applier by getting as much Energy Recharge as possible, which will help her maintain a consistent uptime on her Burst (her main source of Hydro application). For this reason, Candace's weapon and artifact rankings will focus on getting the highest amounts of Particle/Energy generation and Recharge.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Lance: This is going to be Candace's BIS option because of her drastically high ER requirement. If she's the only Hydro character in one team, Candace will need nearly 300% ER to maintain a consistent burst uptime. With another Hydro character to help her out, this ER requirement is lowered down to the 200-250% ER range. This kind of ER requirement is difficult to reach with most polearms, but Favonius Lance makes it possible because each proc of its passive cuts down this requirement by 20% (1 proc --> -20% ER req). Note that with this polearm equipped, Crit Rate also needs to be prioritized on Candace to ensure that its passive is constantly procced.
Engulfing Lightning: This polearm offers a large amount of ER%, which makes it the next best option for Candace after Favonius Lance.
Staff of Homa and Deathmatch: These weapons can be considered if you want Candace to have high personal damage, which is usually notable at [C6]
"The Catch": This polearm is going to be Candace's strongest F2P option, due to its high amount of ER and burst-enhancing capabilities.

Regarding Artifact Choices: 
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4):  Even if Candace is not from Natlan, she can still take advantage of the 12% DMG Bonus buff to the team, alongside the Flat Energy refund from  Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (2) if there is a Nightsoul Burst being activated on the team.
Emblem of Severed Fate (4) and Emblem of Severed Fate (2) + Hydro Damage Bonus/Noblesse (2): With Candace, the only "necessary" stat from her artifacts is the 20% ER you get from EOSF's 2-piece effect. Emblem of Severed Fate's 4-piece effect allows her to better capitalize on the high amounts of ER% that you'll be equipping on her, which makes it her strongest option. However, EoSF is a highly contested artifact set and thus it is possible for you to end up using the 2-piece + 2-piece alternatives listed above.

builds.tips:
Chasca's bullets follow the 'last in first out' rule, meaning the last loaded bullet hits first, while the anemo bullet in slot 1 hits last.

Chasca ideally needs 3 different types of PHEC (Pyro/Hydro/Electro/Cryo) to maximize the conversion rate and Damage Bonus from her passive Bullet Trick.

Ideal Combo*: 4[CA6]

**Number after 'CA' refers to the number of Bullets loaded

notes:
DPS
Chasca is a DPS that focuses on dealing damage through her converted Shining Shadowhunt Shells. Her Anemo damage is considerably low, therefore she is best played with multiple different PHEC (Pyro/Hydro/Electro/Cryo) elements present on the team to enable reactions and convert as many Shells as possible.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Amos' Bow: As Chasca's Shining Shadowhunt Shells are not a projectile and therefore have no travel time, she does not benefit from any stacks of this weapon's passive. It is still a good Stat stick nonetheless.
Polar Star: In theory, this weapon performs slightly better than Amos' Bow. However, due to its high Crit Rate substat, optimizing builds around this weapon is very hard.
Scion of the Blazing Sun: At [R5], this weapon performs close to Skyward Harp.
Song of Stillness: Assumes permanent uptime on the passive. If the team cannot provide frequent heals, this weapon should not be considered.
Flower-Wreathed Feathers: At [R5], this weapon performs similarly to Skyward Harp.
Range Gauge: This weapon needs reliable healing to perform well. With frequent, guaranteed healing instances, this weapon performs slightly better than Song of Stillness [R5], and slightly better than Amos' Bow at [R5].

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Marechaussee Hunter (4): Only useful when paired with Furina, in which case it performs very close to Obsidian Codex (4).

Detailed comparisons here.

notes:
SUPPORT
Chevreuse provides various character buffs and enemy debuffs when party members trigger Overload. The main function of her kit, which is the Pyro and Electro RES reduction only activates if the entire team consists of Pyro and Electro characters. Her buff caps at 40,000 HP after which she no longer needs to build HP.

Chevreuse does not always need to use her Burst, as such Energy Recharge is not needed if she chooses not to use her Burst.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Lance: Loses some value if Chevreuse does not Burst. Still valuable for team Energy.
Symphonist of Scents: Bear in mind you will need around 31% HP from substat rolls with HP% / HP% / HP% Main Stats to cap out her A4 passive using this, which makes it difficult to build enough Energy Recharge to use her burst every rotation.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Noblesse Oblige (4): Only use this set if you use Chevreuse's Burst every rotation.

Regarding Main Stats Priority:
Healing Bonus: Only use this if Chevreuse has already reached 40,000 HP.

Regarding Sub Stats Priority:
Crit Rate: Only if using Favonius Lance.

SUPPORT AND DAMAGE
Support and Damage Chevreuse aims to deal high amounts of damage with her Burst and Skill while maintaining her supportive abilities in Pyro Electro teams. This playstyle is made viable after [C4] because of her ability to use multiple Skills per rotation.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Lance: Though ranked lower for her own damage, Favonius' team Energy regen can make it a desirable option even if it means having lower Chevreuse damage.

notes:
BURST NUKE
It is preferred to use Chongyun as a Burst Nuke over Main DPS as his Normal Attack scalings are very low, even when including Constellation 1. His Elemental Burst on the other hand hits extremely hard on later constellations and takes a much higher total damage portion over his Normal Attack. On top of that, you can Reverse Melt all 3 hits of his Elemental Burst on enemies applied with strong Pyro, such as Bennett's Elemental Skill and Elemental Burst.

At [C2], his Elemental Skill reduces ability cooldown that is cast within the zone by 15%. This is one of the few abilities in the game that can reduce cooldown.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Akuoumaru: At [R1], Akuoumaru beats Luxurious Sea-Lord if Chongyun is used in a team with 260 or greater total team energy. Akuoumaru only beats Luxurious Sea-Lord if [R2] and above otherwise.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Noblesse Oblige (4): Chongyun is one of the best Noblesse Oblige (4) holders since he has low energy requirements for his Elemental Burst.

INFUSION SUPPORT
Chongyun as an infusion support is rather niche, since it is only used to enable on-field Cryo characters that don't already have Cryo infusions. The most notable of these is Cryo DPS Freminet.

Detailed weapon comparisons here.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Thrilling Tales of Dragon Slayers and Wandering Evenstar: At high refinements, Wandering Evenstar can be a better option if the team has multiple attack scaling units with significant damage share. In hypercarry style teams, Thrilling Tales of Dragon Slayers tends to be better.
Other Weapons: While Thrilling Tales of Dragon Slayers and Wandering Evenstar options are preferred due to providing ATK buffs, these weapons can be a great option if the team damage doesn't scale off of ATK.

builds.tips:
Skill Combos:

With Burst:

Q > E > E > 5N3E > N2 or N3

Q > E > 6N3E

Without Burst:

E > 6N3E

N = Normal Attack; E = Elemental Skill; Q = Elemental Burst

notes:
DPS
Clorinde is an Electro DPS who primarily deals damage with her Elemental Skill, which puts her into the Night Vigil state. Attacks during this state grant her a Bond of Life, and she can clear her Bond of Life by using her Elemental Skill again while in the Night Vigil state. It should be noted that while in the Night Vigil state, outside healing sources give Clorinde a Bond of Life instead of healing her. Clorinde's Elemental Burst also grants her a Bond of Life that increases based on her talent level.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Haran Geppaku Futsu, Primordial Jade Cutter: Be careful when using these weapons as it is easy to overcap on Crit Rate due to Clorinde's A4 talent giving her a free 20% Crit Rate.
Mistsplitter Reforged: Maximizing Stacks requires Clorinde to use her Elemental Burst before her Elemental Skill.
Freedom-Sworn: This weapon loses significant value outside of Dendro-based teams.
Calamity of Eshu: Ranking assumes Clorinde is being shielded by someone, most notably being Kirara.
The Black Sword: At [R5], this weapon will perform better than Finale of the Deep [R5].

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Thundering Fury (4): This set pulls ahead of Fragment of Harmonic Whimsy if Clorinde is played in Quickbloom with Furina, and is competitive with Fragment of Harmonic Whimsy in Aggravate.

Regarding Main Stat Choices:
Sands: In Quickbloom, EM sands performs similarly to ATK% sands, and has a lower priority in Aggravate.

Regarding Substats:
Elemental Mastery: In Quickbloom scenarios, EM has a similar priority to ATK%, and has a lower priority in Aggravate. It should not be built outside of Dendro-based teams.
Energy Recharge: Having ER rolls can allow Clorinde to be able to Burst every rotation, which is useful, however, ER should not be prioritized.

Regarding Talent Priority:
It is recommended to bring Clorinde's Elemental Burst talent to at least Level 7 to take full advantage of her A4 talent. This also allows for the Burst rotations as seen in the Ability Tips section.

notes:
HIGH ENERGY OFF-FIELD DPS & BUFF SUPPORT
Columbina blesses her team with a large buff to Lunar reactions, while also inflicting good amounts of Lunar damage herself, mimicking whatever lunar reaction is dominant in your team.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Codex: This weapon is an option if the whole team is desperate for the Energy. For Columbina herself, this weapon is outclassed by Prototype Amber in all regards.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Silken Moon Serenade and Aubade of Morningstar and Moon: Both of these sets can be Columbina's best in slot, the latter being preferred for her personal damage if another unit on the team already holds Silken Moon Serenade.

LOW ENERGY OFF-FIELD DPS & BUFF SUPPORT

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Sacrificial Jade: With refines, this weapon pulls far ahead of similarly ranked options.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Silken Moon Serenade and Aubade of Morningstar and Moon: Both of these sets can be Columbina's best in slot, the latter being preferred for her personal damage if another unit on the team already holds Silken Moon Serenade.

ON-FIELD DPS
Columbina has potential as an On-Field DPS, most notably in Lunar Bloom teams thanks to her special Charged Attack which consumes Verdant Dew to deal Lunar Bloom damage.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Reliquary of Truth: If Columbina can consistently trigger Lunar Bloom for the full passive, this weapon performs close to Nocturne's Curtain Call.
Blackmarrow Lantern: This weapon should only be used if the team is centered around Lunar Bloom.
Sacrificial Jade: At [R5], this weapon performs on par with Surf's Up and Tome of the Eternal Flow.

notes:
ON FIELD DPS
On Field DPS Dehya relies on using her Elemental Skill and Elemental Burst to do damage. 

Regarding Weapon Choice:
Serpent Spine: Dehya has a hard time keeping up this weapon's stacks. At [R5], this weapon can perform better than Wolf's Gravestone.
Mailed Flower: This weapon is only useful in Melt or Vaporize teams and should not be considered otherwise.
Rainslasher: Only useful in Vaporize teams. At [R5], this weapon performs similar to Redhorn Stonethresher.
Tidal Shadow: Assumes Dehya is being healed.
Talking Stick: Assumes Dehya is affected by Pyro. At [R5], this weapon performs better than Tidal Shadow [R5].
A Thousand Blazing Suns: As Dehya needs to place her Skill at the very start of the rotation to not suffer from very high Energy Recharge requirements, she often cannot have this weapon's passive active during her Burst. In teams where 10 seconds pass between her Skill and Burst use, this weapon performs similar to Redhorn Stonethresher.
Akuoumaru: At [R5], this weapon performs better than Wolf's Gravestone.

Regarding Artifacts:
Unfinished Reverie (4): This is Dehya's best set if played in a team with Burning present.
Marechaussee Hunter (4): With Furina on the team, this set performs similar to or better than Emblem of Severed Fate (4), depending on Dehya's Energy Recharge requirement.

SUPPORT
Support Dehya uses her Skill to do passive damage and apply Pyro, while providing the team with interruption resistance and tank damage.

Regarding Weapon Choice:
Sacrificial Greatsword [R5]: For some teams, this weapon can be crucial to enable shorter rotation times.

Regarding Artifacts:
Instructor (4): A useful set if the team appreciates Elemental Mastery.
Deepwood Memories (4): This set is only useful in teams that are focused on Dendro damage, including Bloom-based teams.

REACTION DPS
Reaction DPS Dehya uses her skill to trigger reactions like Overload or Burgeon while off field.

Regarding Weapon Choice:
Sacrificial Greatsword [R5]: For some teams, this weapon can be crucial to enable shorter rotation times.

builds.tips:
Diluc has a number of combos he can do during his uptime. 
N = Normal Attack, E = Elemental Skill, Q = Elemental Burst (N2 would equal 2 Normal Attacks)
His most common combo is Q N2 E N2 E N2 E. This fits perfectly into the buff window of his teams.

For high upfront damage, use E Q N1 E N1 E N1. This falls off significantly if the enemy isn't killed within this window.

A full rotation combo can look similar to Q N2 E N2 E N2 E N4 N3 E N1 E E. An example video can be found here.

notes:
DPS
Regarding Weapon Choices:
These placements assume strong ATK buffs such as Bennett, as Diluc is commonly played with them present in his party.
Beacon of the Reed Sea: This weapon ranking assumes one stack.
Serpent Spine: This placement assumes you can maintain high stacks of the weapon effect. At [R5] it is Diluc's best weapon.
Rainslasher: This weapon should only be used in teams where Diluc is consistently Vaporizing his attacks. This weapon scales well with refinements, potentially surpassing Wolf's Gravestone at [R5].
Tidal Shadow: This ranking assumes Diluc can consistently be healed. Otherwise, this weapon ranks below Earth Shaker.
Blackcliff Slasher: Assume weapon passive does not activate

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Crimson Witch of Flames (4): Generally the best go to set for Diluc due to how easily Diluc can stack the Pyro Damage effect, while his burst imbues his Normal/Charged Attacks with Pyro.
Marechaussee Hunter (4): Competitive with Crimson Witch of Flames (4) when using Furina alongside Diluc. 

Regarding Main Stats Priority:
EM sands can be preferred if Diluc is being used in a Vaporize or Melt team.

Detailed weapon comparisons here.

*Crit Rate substats are only recommended when using Favonius Warbow.
builds.tips: Diona's Elemental Skill grants you a Cryo shield which allows cleansing. Every time you swap characters with the shield up, it applies Cryo. This acts as a cleanse.

notes:
SUPPORT
As a support, Diona's typically used for three things; Shielding, Healing, and Cryo particle generation. Pairing Diona with Cryo damage dealers such as Ayaka, Ganyu and Eula allows you to funnel energy into them while providing them a shield. Weapons such as Sacrificial Bow and Favonius Warbow greatly increase her potential as a battery.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Sacrificial Bow: Preferred over Favonius Warbow at higher refinements when batterying another Cryo character.
Favonius Warbow: It is recommended to build some Crit Rate in substats to have a higher chance of triggering the bow passive for energy.
Silvershower Heartstrings, Recurve Bow, Sequence of Solitude: These bows will be your best choice for stronger shields, however the Energy Recharge and useful passives of other weapons will generally make them a suboptimal pick.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Noblesse Oblige (4): This set provides an teamwide ATK% buff while also increasing burst damage - Diona does not have the best uptime on this set, however, she may be the best option to use this set.
Instructor (4): This set is a niche option for Diona. It can be considered in teams which value EM and where she can trigger a reaction while on-field. Do keep in mind that Instructor being limited to a 4* set means your stat potential will be lower. 
Tenacity of the Millelith (4): This set is a niche option for Diona. It can be considered in teams including some variants of Ganyu Freeze which have shorter rotation lengths meaning Diona's burst will not be used every rotation, and where you can snapshot the ATK% buff onto Ganyu's burst. 

Regarding Main Stats Priority: 
Full Stack HP% is recommended if you want stronger shields but a weaker heal. You can use Healing Bonus Circlet if you want a nice balance between the two. 

Regarding Talent Priority: 
Prioritise whichever your Diona is geared for (shielder or healer), if built for a balance between the two, level both equally.

builds.tips:
Purity form is Durin's default Elemental Burst, it can be swapped to (if in Denial form) via an E E Q rotation.

Durin's Burst in Purity form hits once per 1s, and can apply Pyro in an AoE every other hit.

notes:
VAPORIZE/MELT OFF-FIELD DPS: 
This playstyle leverages Durin's Denial form to deal massively increased Vaporize and/or Melt damage. This is typically done as an off-field DPS, however, on-field variants of this playstyle exist.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Finale of the Deep: For this ranking it is assumed that Durin is healed, and the Bond of Life is cleared. If this cannot occur, this weapon ranks roughly equal to Moonweaver's Dawn.
Wolf Fang: At [R5] this weapon ranks above Summit Shaper.

Regarding Artifact Choices:
Emblem of Severed Fate (4): This set can be preferred over Gilded Dreams (4) in teams where Durin has notably higher Energy Requirements (usually 150%+).

Detailed weapon comparison here.

BUFF SUPPORT & OFF-FIELD DPS
This playstyle uses Durin's Purity form to decrease the Elemental Resistance of Pyro, Dendro, Electro, Anemo, and Geo when reactions with those element and Pyro occur.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Finale of the Deep: For this ranking it is assumed that Durin is healed, and the Bond of Life is cleared. If this cannot occur, this weapon ranks roughly equal to Moonweaver's Dawn.
Wolf Fang: At [R5] this weapon ranks above Absolution.
Lion's Roar: Calculation assumes 90% Pyro/Electro uptime.
Freedom-Sworn: Ranked conservatively. If the team can benefit from Freedom-Sworn's passive, this weapon can be roughly equal to Absolution.

Regarding Artifact Choices:
Emblem of Severed Fate (4): This set can be competitive with A Day Carved By Rising Winds (4) in teams where Durin has notably higher Energy Requirements (usually 150%+).

Detailed weapon comparison here.

notes:
OFF-FIELD DPS
Escoffier is a DPS that dishes out respectable amounts of Off-Field damage through her Elemental Skill while also serving up a sizeable Cryo and Hydro Resistance shred thanks to her Ascension 4 Talent. Additionally, her Elemental Burst and Ascension 1 Talent allow her to keep her Team well nourished and healed. 

Regarding Artifact Set Choices:
Blizzard Strayer (4):  Against freezable enemies, this set slightly outperforms Golden Troupe (4), however it is way worse without reliable freeze uptime and can be hard to build around with Crit Rate weapons.
Tenacity of the Millelith (4)/Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4): These sets are very bad for Escoffier's personal damage, but provide an additional buff to the team.

HIGH ENERGY REQUIREMENT (1-2 CRYO TEAM MEMBERS)

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Deathmatch: Ranking assumes one enemy. At [R5], this weapon performs better than Staff of Homa.
Favonius Lance: While inferior to a number of weapons in terms of personal damage, this weapon can be very good thanks to helping the team with Energy.
Sacrificer's Staff: At [R5], this weapon performs similar to Engulfing Lightning.
Prospector's Drill: Escoffier can reliably get high stacks starting from second rotation onward. Performs close to Lumidouce Elegy at [R5].
Mountain-Bracing Bolt: At [R5] this weapon performs slightly better than similar ranking options.

LOW ENERGY REQUIREMENT (3-4 CRYO TEAM MEMBERS)

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Deathmatch: Ranking assumes one enemy. At [R5], this weapon performs the same as Lumidouce Elegy.
Sacrificer's Staff: At [R5], this weapon performs close to Calamity Queller.
Prospector's Drill: Escoffier can reliably get high stacks starting from second rotation onward. Performs better than Staff of the Scarlet Sands at [R5].
Mountain-Bracing Bolt: At [R5] this weapon performs slightly better than similar ranking options.
Favonius Lance: This weapon is extremely detrimental for Escoffier's personal damage if she does not have high Energy Recharge Requirements, but can possibly yield more team damage.

Detailed weapon comparisons here.

builds.tips:
Normal combo while no Burst:
NA Spam > Tap Skill > NA Spam > Tap Skill

Burst combo:
Tap Skill > Burst > 4 NA > Hold Skill > 4 NA

Burst combo that generates more energy at the cost of slightly less DPS:
Tap Skill > Burst > Tap Skill > 4 NA > Dash Cancel > 2 NA > Hold Skill > 2 NA

*NA = Normal Attack

notes:
DPS
Eula is an on-field Physical damage dealer who deals a significant portion of her damage through her Elemental Burst. Her Normal Attacks also make up a large portion of her overall damage at low Constellation. Weapon rankings are assuming 130% ER minimum, Superconduct and 4pc Noblesse Oblige buff, otherwise no buffs. 

Regarding Weapon Choice:
Redhorn Stonethresher: This weapon can overtake Wolf's Gravestone in terms of Eula's own damage if team provides enough ATK buffs, but Wolf's Gravestone can make up the damage difference by buffing team damage.
Verdict: Weapon rankings are assuming no crystallize. 
The Unforged: Weapon rankings are assuming no shield. With a shield this weapon performs similarly to Beacon of the Reed Sea and Wolf's Gravestone. 
Akuoumaru: This weapon will outperform Luxurious Sea-Lord at higher refinements. At [R5] it can perform similarly to Verdict and Skyward Pride.
Beacon of the Reed Sea: Weapon ranking assumes one stack. At two stacks, it outperforms other weapon options. 
Tidal Shadow: Weapon rankings are assuming the passive is active. At [R5] this weapon performs similarly to Serpent Spine [R1] at 5 stacks. 
Serpent Spine: Weapon rankings are assuming max stacks, in practice this often means running a shielder. At [R5] this weapon performs similarly to Beacon of the Reed Sea.
Regarding weapons which provide Energy Recharge: In teams with low ER requirements on Eula (such as Triple Cryo + Raiden) these weapons lose some effectiveness. 

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Pale Flame (4): This is Eula's optimal artifact set and it relies on having a condition fulfilled in order to outperform Pale Flame (2) Bloodstained Chivalry (2). If you are able to sustain the conditional buff, you will be able to achieve a 50% Physical DMG Bonus + 18% ATK. Without using your Elemental Burst, you can sustain the Pale Flame's 4 piece set bonus because each stack lasts 7s and it refreshes per stack, and Eula's Elemental Skill cooldown is only 4 seconds when doing 1-taps, therefore you can keep the condition up. When using this set, it is recommended to not use Hold Skill cast until your Lightfall Sword is nearly about to end. This restriction is only for players who don't have Eula [C2+]. Please visit the Ability Tips for her combo.
Pale Flame (2) Bloodstained Chivalry (2): This is a non-conditional, consistent artifact set that provides you a constant 50% Physical DMG bonus. While that by itself is amazing, Pale Flame (4) can outperform this set because it also has another 18% ATK buff and is easily maintained through her low tap skill cooldown.

builds.tips:
Regarding Optimal Rotations

For funneling particles to Faruzan:
Skill --> Charged Shot --> Burst

For funneling particles to other units:
Skill --> Burst --> Charged Shot

At C6 bear in mind the Charged shot portion of the rotation no longer becomes necessary since your burst will start activating the Pressurized Collapses instead.

notes:
SUPPORT
As the premier Anemo support unit for Anemo dps based teams, Faruzan combines both high Anemo damage buffing with Anemo resistance shredding to become a cornerstone of any team that wants to focus on Anemo damage. Though building her means primarily focusing on dealing with her high Energy Requirements.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Elegy for the End: Usually only a better option at higher constellation levels such as [C6] where her energy needs can be met without Favonius passive particles.
Sacrificial Bow: It's worth mentioning that the extra Pressurized Collapse from a second E wont give you any extra particles if it's within her 5.5 second particle generation cooldown.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Important Note: Faruzan's Energy Recharge requirements pre-C6 can be extremely high, so high that it can be difficult to build enough using a 4pc set bonus. As such, using Emblem of Severed Fate (2) with whatever artifact combination will give you the most Energy Recharge is a reasonable option. 
Viridescent Venerer (4): This set should be used in teams which have a significant amount of Pyro/Hydro/Electro/Cryo damage. Note the resistance shred does not buff Anemo damage and the set wielder must Swirl the element(s) while on-field for the resistance shred. 
Noblesse Oblige (4): This is her best option in teams that either won't benefit significantly from Viridescent Venerer (4) shred, or teams that already have another unit holding the aforementioned set.
Tenacity of the Millelith (4): This has similar usage cases to Noblesse Oblige (4), except in that it requires Faruzan C6 for improved uptime through extra Pressurized Collapse activations.
Emblem of Severed Fate (4): This set will give Faruzan her best personal damage output, assuming the team is already running Viridescent Venerer (4). Also if you're willing to invest more time farming substats it can potentially give you more Energy Recharge than running The Exile (2).
Golden Troupe: This set is better than Emblem of Severed Fate (4) if Faruzan is C6 because of the pressurized collapse damage.

builds.tips: When using Blizzard Strayer (4), Ganyu reaches max Crit Rate with a base of 45% on her Charged attacks and a base of 65% on her Elemental Burst. Anything between 45% and 55% Crit Rate is a nice point to be at without sacrificing too much damage on either end.

notes:
MELT DPS
Ganyu's Level 2 Charged Shot applies two instances Cryo as they do not share the same ICD, which allows you to trigger Melt reaction twice for both damage instances. Charged Attack will be the majority of her damage output while neglecting her Skill/Burst damage contribution for most scenarios.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Amos' Bow: Ganyu's Level 2 Charge Shot Bloom extends arrow flight duration allowing her to get 3 stacks of Amos' Bow passive even on point blank range.
Flower-Wreathed Feathers: This weapon scales incredibly well with refinements. At [R5], this weapon performs better than The First Great Magic.
Hamayumi: Due to its passive, this weapon prefers Wanderer's Troupe (4) instead. At higher refinements, Hamayumi can outperform Thundering Pulse and Skyward Harp.
Scion of the Blazing Sun: At [R5], this weapon can outperform Thundering Pulse and Skyward Harp.  
Prototype Crescent: At higher refinement levels with good uptime on its passive, it can potentially be on par with Skyward Harp. However, do bear in mind that not all enemies have weak spots that you can headshot, which does affect its performance significantly. 

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Shimenawa's Reminiscence (4) and Wanderer's Troupe (4): If you are able to squeeze 5 Charged Attacks during the 10 second buff duration, Shimenawa's Reminiscence (4) pulls ahead of Wanderer's Troupe (4). Due to it being very difficult to do, Wanderer's Troupe (4) will usually end up dealing more damage for most players. 
Unfinished Reverie: If Ganyu is played in a team with consistent burn uptime, this set performs better than Wanderer's Troupe (4) and  Shimenawa's Reminiscence (4).

Regarding Talent Priority:
Ganyu's Elemental Burst is low priority on a Melt Build since most of the times, you don't cast her burst at all so that it doesn't steal your Melt reaction.

FREEZE DPS
Ganyu's burst is the main damage source in freeze teams, therefore charged attacks should only be used when the time between using the teams skills and bursts allows it. If running Shenhe alongside Ganyu, weapons with Crit stats and/or DMG% are slightly favored.

Regarding Weapon Choices: 
Polar Star: This weapon is much harder to optimize with, due to having a very high Crit Rate stat.
Amos' Bow: In quickswap heavy rotations (Ganyu Mona Venti Diona) where you usually don't do a lot of Charged Shots, this weapon performs worse than the other 5 star options listed.
Song of Stillness: Ranking assumed Ganyu receives healing before casting her Elemental Burst.
Prototype Crescent: Requires a weak spot hit before casting Ganyu's Elemental Burst to utilize the passive fully. At [R5], this weapon peforms close to Skyward Harp.
Mouun's Moon: This weapon's exact performance varies with the teams total burst cost. At [R5], this weapon performs similar to Thundering Pulse.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Marechaussee Hunter (4): This set should only be used with Furina on the team and performs only slightly worse than Blizzard Strayer (4).

MONO CRYO DPS
Mono Cryo Ganyu teams utilize Blizzard Strayer (4) and Cryo Resonance while having Ganyu on field dealing damage through both her Charged Attacks and Elemental Burst.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Amos' Bow: If Shenhe is present on the team, this weapon performs worse than Skyward Harp.
Flower-Wreathed Feathers; At [R5], this weapon performs similar to Prototype Crescent [R5].
Prototype Crescent: Requires a weak spot hit before casting Ganyu's Elemental Burst to utilize the passive fully. At [R5], this weapon peforms sligthly better (without Shenhe) or slightly worse (with Shenhe) than Skyward Harp.
Mouun's Moon: This weapon's exact performance varies with the teams total burst cost. Performs better than similar options with Shenhe present on the team.
Song of Stillness: Ranking assumed Ganyu receives healing before casting her Elemental Burst.
Flower-Wreathed Feathers: This weapon scales incredibly well with refinements. At [R5], this weapon performs closer to Amos' Bow.

builds.tips: [C2] extends the duration of Gorou's Elemental Burst from 9 seconds to 12 seconds if you can obtain the required Crystallize shields. This allows his teammates to gain the 3 Geo 15% Geo DMG% bonus for a longer duration, so it's preferable to use Burst as your last ability before swapping into your DPS.

notes:
SUPPORT
Gorou is a Support character that can give increasing buffs to your party members from his Elemental Skill and Elemental Burst based on the number of Geo characters in your team (capped at 3). Casting his Elemental Skill sets up a banner which gives flat DEF (1 Geo), Interruption Resistance (2 Geo) and Geo DMG% Bonus (3 Geo) for your active character standing inside the banner radius. Casting his Elemental Burst creates a field following the active character which deals periodic damage and offers the same bonuses given by the Skill, destroying any placed banners in the process, it also pulls in nearby Elemental Shards created by Crystallize reactions. Due to his kit focusing on buffing DEF and Geo DMG, he's a specialised support geared towards Geo DPS that scales with DEF, such as Noelle or Itto. He can see niche use at [C6] with other Geo characters as well.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Elegy for the End: Elegy offers a helpful ER substat alongside an EM and ATK% buff for your teammates. While not directly useful in teams centered around Noelle or Itto, this could possibly be a good choice for future Geo DPS characters, or in a team which utilizes Geo Resonance.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
The Exile (4): Casting Elemental Burst regenerates a total of 6 Energy for each of your party member. This set reduces your Energy Requirements by a bit for all of your party members, which can be quite beneficial. Do note that this is a 4 star set so you're potentially missing out on 5 star stat scaling, which is a 34% difference in Main Stat value and 25% difference in individual substat value.
Noblesse Oblige (4): The effectiveness of this set increases if you've already met the ER requirement for Gorou. At that point, you'd no longer need to run the recommended ER% sets, which means you can opt for this instead. 
Instructor (4): This is a set Gorou may run when used in a Lunar-Crystallize team, notably when paired with Zibai. Because this is a 4-star set, you're losing out on 5-star stat scaling. It is recommended to run an offpiece 5-star sands while running this set.
Silken Moon's Serenade (4): Can be used when Gorou is paired with Columbina or Zibai so that other units can run more beneficial artifact sets.

Regarding Artifact Main Stats and Substats Priority:
DEF% Main Stats and substats (and potentially a Healing Bonus Main Stat on Circlet) can be considered at [C4] due to being able to heal the active character based on his DEF during burst. This is useful if you plan to use him as a solo healer.
Crit Rate circlet and substats can be considered when Gorou is using Favonius Warbow to trigger the passive comfortably.
ER% substats can usually be more important than getting the right Main Stat, as Gorou's damage contribution to a team is negligible, while being able to get Energy for his Burst is a lot more important.

builds.tips:
When ending Hu Tao's combos with an Elemental Burst, if you do not jump/dash cancel the last charged attack, the Elemental Burst will still benefit from Hu Tao's Elemental Skill.

It is recommended to use cancels when performing your Hu Tao combos as to maximize damage.
N2CJ for [C0] and N2CD for [C1].
N3CJ/N3CD can be used to conserve stamina at the cost of damage.

*N = Normal Attack, C = Charged Attack, J = Jump, D = Dash

notes:
DPS
Hu Tao is a predominantly Charged Attack focused DPS who best synergizes in teams with sufficient off-field Hydro application which allow her to Vaporize all of her Charged Attack hits. 

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Staff of the Scarlet Sands: When using this weapon, Gilded Dreams (4) becomes competitive with Crimson Witch of Flames (4) for being Hu Tao's best artifact set.
Ballad of the Fjords and Lithic Spear (4 Stacks): At [R5], these weapons are better than Staff of the Scarlet Sands.
Primordial Jade Winged-Spear: Is better than the [R1] variants of Dragon's Bane and Deathmatch with ideal stacking. While this is a very strong weapon when fully stacked, maintaining the stacks on Hu Tao isn't ideal in realistic situations making the weapon less consistent than other options.
Deathmatch: This weapon is better than Dragon's Bane when you're not doing Vaporize.
Blackcliff Pole: Despite seeming quite good due to the Crit DMG substat, Blackcliff Pole at 1 stack performs only slightly better than an [R5] White Tassel in practice as it is difficult to maintain stacks consistently. It is not recommended to buy this weapon specifically for Hu Tao and should only be used if you already have one.
White Tassel: This weapon prefers an N2C combo over an N1C combo.

Regarding Artifact Sets: 
Shimenawa's Reminiscence (4): Provides higher Normal and Charged Attack damage than Crimson Witch of Flames (4), but reduces Elemental Burst damage, impacting overall AoE damage. Additionally, if you're unable to burst, it might lead to survivability issues unless you're running a healer.
Gilded Dreams (4): At 3 EM stacks, this artifact set is equal to Crimson Witch of Flames (4) in teams where you are not getting EM from other sources (e.g team or weapon). 
Marechaussee Hunter (4): This is Hu Tao's best set when you can reliably get 3 stacks, which typically requires running Furina, or healer that can heal smaller amounts over time (i.e. Xianyun, Chevreuse).

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority: 
Since Hu Tao is able to trigger Vaporize on most of her damage output, Elemental Mastery is quite valuable on her. If you have less than 100EM total (including from Sucrose EM share, Instructor (4), etc) EM substats take priority over Crit Rate / DMG.

Detailed weapon comparisons here.

builds.tips:
Iansan's buff is based on 3 Factors:

1. Iansan's Total ATK
2. # of Iansan's Nightsoul Points
3. "Kinetic Energy Scale"

Iansan can hold a max of 54 Nightsoul Points, and her buff decreases in value when she's below 42 Nightsoul Points. Consistent movement is needed to maintain good buff uptime.

Iansan's [C4] allows her to frontload Nightsoul Points if it had ever overflowed previously.

notes:
SUPPORT
Iansan buffs her teammates through her Elemental Burst, as well as provide some additional healing for the active character through one of her passives. Characters with movement-based abilities will greatly contribute to Iansan's buff as Iansan's own movement cannot contribute to it. Movement is needed to help Iansan maintain a high number of Nightsoul Points, and the active character gaining and consuming Nightsoul Points can also contribute. Movement is defined as anything that moves the active character for any amount of distance, whether vertical or horizontal. This includes things such as attacks, skill and burst animations, jumping, etc. Almost any kind of movement will contribute, as long as it's not Iansan's own. This also extends to the gain and consumption of Nightsoul Points.

Iansan's [C1] gives her 15 Energy upon consuming 6 Nightsoul Points as long as she's in combat and in Nightsoul Blessing state, effectively changing her burst cost from 70 to 55. This has a cooldown of 18 seconds.

Regarding Weapon Choices: It is important to note that at [C0], using non-ER weapons in teams where Iansan is the only Electro may be unrealistic due to how high her ER needs can become. In addition, in teams where movement is scarce, ATK weapons gain greater value.
Favonius Lance: With this weapon, you want to build some Crit Rate to help proc the passive.
Symphonist of Scents: Keep in mind that the fewer Natlan characters you have on your team, the lower the buff’s uptime will be with near perfect uptime usually only achievable when using three or more Natlan characters.

notes:
REACTION DPS
Ifa uses his Elemental Mastery scalings to provide teamwide healing while driving reactions on-field. He can assist in permanently tranquilizing his foes by buffing the Swirl and Electro-Charged reactions, based on the total number of Nightsoul Points shared across the entire team, up to a maximum of 150 points (200 if Ifa is [C2]). For this reason, it is highly recommended to pair Ifa with Ororon. 

Regarding Weapon Choices: Weapon options below Mappa Mare do not improve Ifa's healing capabilities.
Sacrificial Fragments: While having a similar DMG output as Mappa Mare, this weapon will provide more total healing over your rotations.
Thrilling Tales of Dragon Slayers: This weapon is a support option to help boost the ATK% of your other off-field DPS's, notably Fischl, Xingqiu, and Ororon.

ANEMO DPS
This build focuses on Ifa's Anemo Damage.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Cashflow Supervision: When HP loss is present (notably with Furina), this weapon becomes Ifa's best in slot.
The Widsith: At [R5], this weapon will perform roughly equal to Surf's Up! and its similar ranking options.

Regarding Artifact Choices:
Viridescent Venerer (4): Prioritize this set if no one else holds it.

Detailed weapon comparisons here.

2. Elemental Mastery
3. Crit Rate**

*Prioritize Energy Recharge until requirements are met

builds.tips:
Lightkeeper's Oath does not apply to Illuga himself.

Nightingale's Song starts with 21 stacks, and for every Geo Construct that is on the field when his Burst is used, or if Geo Constructs are created during his Burst, Nightingale's Song gains 5 stacks, up to a maximum of 15 additional stacks.

The Moondrifts that are created by the Lunar-Crystallize reaction count as Geo Constructs.

notes:
OFF-FIELD BUFF SUPPORT
Illuga buffs Geo DMG and Lunar-Crystallize DMG after using his Elemental Burst, which grants the Nightingale's Song effect. This buff scales on Illuga's Elemental Mastery, and with his A4 talent, is further amplified depending on how many Geo and Hydro characters are in the party. With his A1 talent, using either his Skill or Burst will grant other team members the Lightkeeper's Oath effect, which boosts the Crit Rate and DMG of Geo DMG, and if Ascendant Gleam is active, boost the character's Elemental Mastery as well.

Illuga's [C1] grants him 12 Energy if he triggers a Geo-related reaction while on-field, effectively changing his Burst cost from 60 to 48. This has a cooldown of 15 seconds.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Dragon's Bane: An Energy Recharge sands may be necessary if you are unable to get enough ER from substats alone.

Regarding Artifacts:
Silken Moon's Serenade (4): This set requires [C2] Illuga.

builds.tips:
Birgitta stays on-field longer than both of Ineffa's Elemental Skill and Burst individual cooldowns, meaning that Birgitta has 100% field uptime.

When Ineffa has higher Energy Recharge requirements (typically 140%+), building ER to use her Burst every rotation results in a personal damage loss in exchange for more uptime on her teamwide buff. Teams with Ineffa as a significant source of damage will want to use her Burst every other rotation and ignore ER.

notes:
DPS & SUPPORT
Ineffa transforms all instances of the Electro-Charged reaction into Lunar-Charged while she's in the team, which has its own scalings and can even crit. Both her Elemental Skill and Elemental Burst deploy her robot companion, Birgitta, to the field, who can deal damage and apply Electro while off-field. Additionally, Ineffa's Elemental Skill can also provide a shield. Her Elemental Burst also provides an Elemental Mastery buff based on a portion of Ineffa's ATK (her A4 talent).

Weapon calculations can be found here. 

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Staff of the Scarlet Sands: Can sometimes utilize EM Main Stats.
Ballad of the Fjords: At [R5] this weapon will perform closer to Primordial Jade Winged-Spear.
Prospector's Shovel: This ranking assumes Ineffa is used on a team where Moonsign: Ascendant Gleam is active. Otherwise it performs slightly worse than Skyward Spine.
Deathmatch: Ranking assumes single target scenarios. At [R5], this weapon performs equal to Bloodsoaked Ruins.

Regarding Artifacts:
Tenacity of the Millelith: This is a more supportive artifact set that sacrifices Ineffa's personal damage in favor of an ATK% buff and an increase to her shield strength.

notes:
SUPPORT AND DAMAGE
Jean's Elemental Skill can be used to fling smaller mobs into the air to deal fall damage, and to stagger bigger ones. Her healing scales with ATK.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Amenoma Kageuchi: This weapon can lower your ER% requirements, depending on the number of stacks on the passive and the refinement of this weapon. As such, at higher refines / more stacks on the passive, this weapon can perform better than Skyward Blade, while at lower refines / less stacks, it can perform worse.
Lion's Roar: Depending on passive uptime, it can be ranked much higher in terms of Jean's personal damage.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Viridescent Venerer (4): The bread and butter for Anemo characters in general, as it is commonly used for the 40% elemental resistance shred.
Noblesse Oblige (4): Another option for providing utilities to the team. This set gives a 20% partywide ATK boost after Jean casts her Elemental Burst.
Ocean-Hued Clam (4): The best set for Jean's personal damage, as the damage it deals scales with Jean's burst healing. However, because Jean's total damage output is on the low end, it is generally recommended to prioritize utility options such as Noblesse Oblige (4) or Viridescent Venerer (4) over this set.

Regarding Main Stats Priority:
While typically Jean will heal enough that you do not need to try and build for as much healing as possible, when paired with Furina it can be beneficial swapping to an ATK% goblet and Healing Bonus circlet. This is to aid in building Fanfare points faster, and Jean's personal damage is not particularly significant to begin with. When building Jean in such a way, prioritize weapons which provide ATK% or Energy Recharge. If using Favonius Sword, a Crit Rate circlet can be taken to ensure more consistent procs. 

Regarding Talent Priorities: 
If you prefer more damage than healing, prioritize Elemental Skill over Elemental Burst since it scales better.
For Support Jean, you can either go for more ATK for stronger heals, but relatively lower Burst uptime, or more ER for lower heals, but higher Burst uptime. Choosing between these two is up to personal preference, although if another Anemo character is on the team, Jean has very few energy problems and can potentially go for a full Sub DPS build.

REACTION DPS
Reaction DPS Jean relies on her unique interaction regarding her Elemental Burst. If the active character if afflicted by Hydro / Pyro / Electro / Cryo, Jean's Burst will infuse them with Anemo and create a swirl trigger around the character. This interaction can be abused by using Bennett to consistently infuse the active character with Pyro. This allows for an extremely high amount of Pyro Swirl triggers. These swirl's damage scales off of Jean's Elemental Mastery.

builds.tips:
Turbo Twirly is considered a Geo Construct.

Kachina's Burst can bring Turbo Twirly back to her location if it is already on the field, allowing for a quick method of repositioning without having to actually ride Turbo Twirly. Her [C2] will summon a new Turbo Twirly if one isn't on the field instead.

notes:
OFF-FIELD SUPPORT
As a free unit from Natlan, Kachina's value comes from being able to provide Off-Field Geo Application with her Elemental Skill, and being able to hold Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4) to great effect. Kachina's Elemental Burst can extend the radius in which Turbo Twirly creates shockwaves, allowing you to hit enemies from a distance if you need to. While she can be built for damage, her personal scalings are rather low and building for damage isn't a requirement.

Kachina's [C1] allows her to restore 3 Energy whenever she picks up a Crystalize Shard, and mounting/dismounting Turbo Twirly absorbs nearby shards. The energy restoration can only trigger once every 5 seconds, but does help alleviate some of her Energy needs.

Regarding Mainstat and Substat priority:
If you are building Kachina as a low investment support, using the correct mainstats is not important. Crit Rate should be prioritised further in this case when using Favonius Lance for more consistent triggers. 

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Footprint of the Rainbow: Kachina can opt to use this weapon if her ER needs are satisfied and the team does not value the energy from Favonius Lance.  Refinements are unnecessary unless you wish to see more out of Kachina's personal damage.

Regarding Artifact Choices:
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City should be the top priority for Kachina, unless there is another unit in the same team that wants the set more, then you can use the other sets listed.

notes:
FREEZE / MONO CRYO

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Haran Geppaku Futsu: This Weapon performs slightly better than Light of Foliar Incision if Kaeya is played on-field with Chongyun in the team.
Primordial Jade Cutter: If running Blizzard Strayer (4), it is impossible not to overcrit with this weapon in Freeze teams and very hard not to overcrit in Mono Cryo teams. It is advised to switch either the weapon or the set in this case.
Finale of the Deep: If you can reliably clear bond of life before casting burst, this weapon performs better than Splendor of Tranquil Waters.
Amenoma Kageuchi, Skyward Blade and Favonius Sword: These weapons have higher value in teams where Kaeya faces high Energy Recharge Requirements.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Noblesse Oblige: This set undermines Kaeya's personal Damage, but can contribute more overall team Damage thanks to the buff it provides.

REVERSE MELT
Kaeya is both a good DPS and battery in Reverse Melt teams. His Damage contribution comes from both Elemental Burst hits as well as occasional melted Skills.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Uraku Misugiri: With a Geo unit on the team, this weapon can perform similarly to Mistsplitter Reforged if Kaeya melts two or more Skills per rotation.
Lion's Roar: At [R5], this weapon performs similar to Absolution.
Finale of the Deep: If you can reliably clear bond of life before casting burst, this weapon performs better than Summit Shaper.
Skyward Blade: In teams without a second Cryo unit, this weapon increases in value.
Favonius Sword: This weapon can be preferred over other options to help the team meet Energy Recharge requirements.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Instructor (4) and Noblesse Oblige (4): These sets undermine Kaeya's personal Damage, but can contribute more overall team Damage thanks to the buffs they provide.

builds.tips:
Ayaka's Charged Attack, Elemental Skill and Elemental Burst is able to hit enemies inside sucked inside Venti's Elemental Burst.

Her Elemental Burst also stays in place on frozen enemies and slightly knocks enemies away when they're not frozen.

notes:
DPS
Kamisato Ayaka is a pretty straightforward DPS that deals most of her damage from her Elemental Burst. She has very high scalings on her burst at the cost of it being 80 energy. You will likely need another Cryo battery on the team to provide energy for her (Diona, Kaeya, Rosaria, etc). She has a special dash (like Mona) and after she dashes, she will get a Cryo infusion on her Normal Attacks. Ensure you always have this bonus active to maximise your rotation damage.

Regarding Main Stats Priority:
Circlet main stat will be dependant on your overall stats, depending on your overall stats, one can be better than the other. Since Ayaka is able to get a lot of Crit Value from Blizzard Strayer (4) set, ascension passive, and also possibly weapons, ATK% circlets can be competitive to Crit ones, it also has a better drop rate, so don't throw away your good ATK% circlets.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Primordial Jade Cutter: With Blizzard Strayer (4) and Cryo Resonance, this weapon overcrits, so try to avoid getting Crit Rate substats.
Finale of the Deep: Weapon rankings are assuming you clear the Life Bond. If you cannot clear the Life Bond, it performs similarly to Amenoma Kageuchi.
Aquila Favonia: While Aquila Favonia has Physical Damage% as a main stat, which is basically useless on Ayaka, it is still a good statstick as it has a high base ATK and ATK% buff from the passive, however, it's not a huge upgrade over the other 4 star options and there might be a character which can utilize this weapon better (such as Bennett).
Skyward Blade: The Energy Recharge main stat provided from this sword makes Ayaka comfortable in terms of having a good burst uptime, try to avoid getting Energy Recharge substats since the weapon already provides you with enough Energy Recharge.
Amenoma Kageuchi: Lowers Ayaka's Energy Recharge requirement which allows her to allocate some of her Energy Recharge substats to other offensive substats. 
Harbinger of Dawn: A good Crit statstick if you can keep the requirements up (having >90% HP).

builds.tips:
Try to activate Ayato's Elemental Burst before using his skill to maximize his Normal Attack DMG. Getting enough Energy Recharge for this is important, and varies based on team. Since the casting time is so long, it may not be worth snapshotting into buffs.

The amount of slashes Ayato can do during an Elemental Skill window is sometimes influenced by the player's set FPS.

notes:
DPS
DPS Ayato focuses on doing large amounts of on-field damage with his Elemental Skill and Elemental Burst. 

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Skyward Blade: This ranking assumes Ayato needs 135% Energy Recharge to use his Burst every rotation. Its ranking changes based on his Energy Recharge Requirements.
Summit Shaper: In teams where ATK buffs are scarce, this weapon has the potential to rival The Black Sword.
Finale of the Deep: If you can reliably clear the Bond of Life, this weapon is better than the weapons ranked equal to it.
The Black Sword: At [R5] this weapon is roughly equal to Summit Shaper. 
Lion's Roar: In Electro-Charged teams, this weapon performs about equal to Summit Shaper.
Harbinger of Dawn: Only recommended with a shielder. If shielded, this weapon is better than Finale of the Deep and can rival Splendor of Tranquil Waters in teams with high amounts of ATK buffs.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Echoes of an Offering (4): The performance of this artifact set is inconsistent, and is based on user ping and other factors. It is not recommended to run this set unless your ping is consistently below 100. If you can achieve this, Echoes of an Offering (4) is competitive with Heart of Depth (4).
Marechaussee Hunter (4): If Ayato is used in a Burgeon team where he consistently takes damage, or with Furina, this is Ayato's best set.
Nymph's Dream (4): Reliant on Ayato's Water Illusion hitting enemies which can be inconsistent against some content. Without the third stack of the passive, this set becomes about equal to +15% Hydro DMG set (2) +18% ATK set (2).
Blizzard Strayer (4): When run in a Freeze Team, this is Ayato's best set. Note that it is not recommended to farm specifically for this set.
Thundering Fury (4): When run in an Electro-Charged team, this is Ayato's best set due to the Cooldown Reduction on Elemental Skill allowing for shorter rotations. Note that it is not recommended to farm specifically for this set.

builds.tips: When paired with Xiangling, spin counter clockwise so her Pyronado can hit opponents more frequently.

notes:
DPS
Kinich is a Dendro DPS who relies on tethering himself to enemies with his grappling hook and firing Loop Shots to generate Nightsoul Points in order to use the Scalespiker Cannon. Both the Loop Shots and the Scalespiker Cannon are considered Elemental Skill DMG. With his A1 Talent, his skill puts enemies in the Desolation State, which, when they are affected by Burning or Burgeon, generates even more Nightsoul Points for Kinich. Summoning Ajaw to the field will extend his Nightsoul Blessing state briefly.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Rankings assume Kinich is used with Bennett.
Serpent Spine: Performs much worse without a shielder. At [R5], this weapon performs better than Beacon of the Reed Sea.
Earth Shaker: Earth Shaker is an inconsistent weapon choice in teams only consisting of Dendro and Pyro characters. Since only the activation of Burning counts toward the passive, meaning that the passive activation can only trigger once per enemy.
Wolf's Gravestone: This option is mainly just a statstick for Kinich as many of his teammates, barring Xiangling, Emilie, Mavuika, or Xingqiu, don't benefit much from the ATK% buff from the passive.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Obsidian Codex (4) / Unfinished Reverie (4): These two sets are about equal to each other. The less DMG% Kinich has from his weapon and/or team, the better Unfinished Reverie (4) performs. Inversely, the more DMG% Kinich already has, the better Obsidian Codex (4) performs relatively.
Marechaussee Hunter (4): Only to be used when paired with Furina, or when played in Burgeon. Performs similarly to Unfinished Reverie (4).

Detailed weapon comparisons here.

builds.tips:
Klee has access to a number of animation cancels that allow her to deal damage with her talents faster than normal. 

Her A1 grants her "Explosive Spark" stacks when she does a Normal Attack, Skill or Burst that when consumed, give her Charged Attacks extra DMG% and not consume Stamina.

Hexerei effects give her up to 150% DMG to her Enhanced Charged Attacks - this effect should always be active at max stacks.

Her optimal combos include N1C/N3 in teams where hexerei resonance is active, or N1C/N1W in teams where it isn't.

notes:
DPS
Being extremely squishy and prone to interruptions, Klee requires great positioning, or the use of a shielder - This is partically mitigated in her best teams currently, which focus on Overload with Chevreuse, also provide amazing stagger that helps her immensely with not getting hit. Even with this consideration, her best teams nomally don't include a shielder, she can often get knocked around and even KO'd in the midst of your rotations. Running a shielder makes survivability with Klee more comfortable, but at the cost of total DPS. You can choose teammates depending on your level of skill and comfortability.

Regarding Weapon Choices: Weapon rankings assume Hexerei Resonance is active. 
The Widsith: At [R5], this weapon performs closer to Tulaytullah's Remembrance. Klee cannot reliably utilize the Elemental Mastery buff, should she acquire it.
Solar Pearl: At [R5], this weapon performs slightly worse than The Widsith [R5].  
Flowing Purity: Calculations assume you clear the BoL.
Lost Prayer to the Sacred Winds: Assumed 12 DMG% averaged.
Kagura's Verity: Assumed 2 stacks on passive.
Tulaytullah's Remembrance: Averaged 25 Normal Attack DMG%.
Surf's Up: Dynamic stacking.
Flowing Purity [R5]: This weapon is calculated with the assumption that the BoL is instantly cleared. If this weapon is paired with Fragment of Harmonic Whimsy (4) at max stacks, this weapon can perform as well as Tullaytullah's Remembrance.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Lavawalker (4): Klee's kit makes it harder to utilize Vaporize and Melt the same way another Pyro carry would, but her constant Pyro application makes Lavawalker (4) a viable set.
Desert Pavillion Chronicle: Klee naturally use her Charged Attack early on in the rotation making Desert Pavillion Chronicle (4) a viable set.
Echoes of an Offering (4): With a 50.2% proc chance on average that gives Flat DMG to her Normal Attack, this set is competitive to Lavawalker (4) and is easier to farm.
Fragment of Harmonic Whimsy (4): This becomes Klee's second best set when you are specifically using Flowing Purity. For this reason, this set not a set you should farm for and is only recommended if you already have the set on standby.

builds.tips:
For her Elemental Skill to generate particles, you need to perform a Charged Shot to place a Crowfeather after casting her Elemental Skill. If the Crowfeather explosion hits an enemy, it generates 3 particles.

Crowfeathers dropped through Sara's [C2] do NOT generate particles.

notes:
SUPPORT AND DAMAGE
Sara is a quickswap support who is able to buff your active character via her Crowfeather mechanic while dealing a decent amount of personal damage. When Sara uses her Elemental Skill, her next Charged Shot will leave a Crowfeather at the target location which explodes after a short while, granting the active character in its AoE an attack buff based on Sara's Base ATK. Active characters within her Burst's AoE will also receive the same buff or have its duration refreshed. 
If building Sara for personal damage, it is recommended to let her receive the buff provided by her Skill to enhance her Burst, while letting the next active character be buffed by her Burst.

At [C2], you can skip the Charged Shot as it immediately drops a Crowfeather, and save it for later on if you wanna swap back to her to extend her buffs. The Crit Damage buff provided by Sara's [C6] cannot be snapshot and is not shown in the attribute page.

Regarding Weapon Choices: Weapons here are ranked based on Team DPS to account for Sara's Buff and Personal Damage. 
Skyward Harp: This weapon combines the highest base attack for Sara's buff with good stats for her personal damage, making it the best overall pick.
Polar Star: This weapon trades some buff strength for more personal damage. Can pull ahead of Skyward Harp if a quick E CA N1 Q rotation is performed to maximize its passive.
Elegy for the End: While providing a strong buff for the team and a large amount of Energy Recharge, Sara can have issues with reliably activating the passive in Single Target scenarios against small or mobile enemies.
Mouun's Moon: At [R5], this weapon performs similar to or better than Thundering Pulse, depending on your team's total burst cost.
Alley Hunter: Sara's quickswap playstyle lets her take advantage of this weapon's passive well. Performs similar to Amos' Bow at [R5].
Favonius Warbow: While being bad for both Sara's buff and personal damage, this weapon can be a good pick if the team struggles with high Energy Recharge requirements.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Noblesse Oblige (4): A good pick for Sara if there is no other possible holder on the team, or if it allows for another buffing set to be added to the team.
