builds.weapons:
1. Aqua Simulacra (5✩)
2. Silvershower Heartstrings (5✩)
3. Mouun's Moon (4✩) [R3-R5]* 
≈ The Stringless (4✩) [R3-R5]
≈ Slingshot (3✩) 
≈ Sequence of Solitude (4✩) [R5]
4. Hunter's Path (5✩)*
≈ Polar Star (5✩)*
≈ Thundering Pulse (5✩)*
≈ Skyward Harp (5✩)*
5. Fading Twilight (4✩) [R5] 
6. Recurve Bow (3✩)

notes:
OFF-FIELD DPS
Yelan's primary role is as an off-field Hydro DPS who can also enable reactions thanks to her elemental application. Similarly to Xingqiu, her exquisite throws from Elemental Burst require your character to perform the Normal Attack action.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Hunter's Path / Polar Star / Thundering Pulse / Skyward Harp: These weapons perform slightly better than Slingshot, however as Yelan does not care about Base ATK and the passives are less than ideal for her, it is not recommended to pull or prioritize using these weapons on Yelan over Slingshot if you have characters who could benefit more from them. 
Elegy for the End: This weapon is not ideal for Yelan's personal damage so should only be used in teams which can utilise the buff well. 
Mouun's Moon: This weapon works best at [R3+] and makes HP% Goblet potentially a stronger option than Hydro DMG% in teams with significant DMG% buffing.
Sacrificial Bow: This ideally needs to be at refinement [R3-R5] to function properly in most Yelan teams rotations without desyncing the rotation and extending her field time.
Aqua Simulacra: Still the best option for personal damage even at high ER requirements, but may be unfeasible to run in some teams as the ER requirement can become very difficult to reach without an ER weapon. 

Regarding Artifact Sets and Main Stats:
Marechaussee Hunter (4): This set should only be used for on-field Yelan with Furina, but in such cases it is the strongest set option. 
Goblet: Hydro DMG% should be used by default. In teams with significant DMG% buffing or with weapons which provide a lot of DMG%, HP% goblet can be competitive. 
Additionally, it's never recommended to run more than 2 HP% Main Stat artifacts at a time.

Regarding Talent Priority:
Normal Attacks: Level this up only if you want to constantly use her Breakthrough Barbs.

Big thanks to Vladone ツ#9281 for helping out

builds.tips: Yoimiya's hardest hitting part of her Normal Attack string is her N5, and in Vaporize teams you should be Vaporizing the N1 (first hit), N3 and N5.

notes:
DPS
Yoimiya is an on-field Pyro DPS who specializes in dealing damage through her normal attacks. It is recommended to play Yoimiya with some source of resistance to interruption, as breaking her normal attack string is a significant damage loss. This ranking assumes a vaporize team with Bennett.

Regarding Weapon Choices: 
Rust: Scales well with refinements, at [R5] this weapon performs similar to Polar Star. At [R4+] it outperforms Slingshot [R5]
Polar Star: Assumes 2 stacks by performing a quick aimed shot before using skill.
Amos' Bow: Weapon rankings are assuming 3 stacks. At 2 stacks it performs worse than Slingshot [R5]. The closer you are to the enemy, the less stacks this weapon gets.


Regarding Artifacts:  
Gilded Dreams (4): This artifact set should only be used in teams where Yoimiya frequently triggers elemental reactions.
Echoes of an Offering (4): An okay choice if you have a good set already, however not recommended to farm as it is very inconsistent and becomes even less useful if your ping is >100 or generally jumping a lot.

2. Energy Recharge
3. Flat DEF

notes:
SUPPORT
Yun Jin is a Support character for Normal Attack focused characters who can deal off-field damage via her Elemental Burst. Yun Jin's Elemental Burst grants nearby party members 30 stacks of additional flat damage bonus on Normal Attacks which scales with Yun Jin's current DEF. This means that external DEF buffs like from Gorou's Elemental Skill and/or Burst doesn't provide much to Yun Jin since it only buffs active characters and not off-field ones.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Lance: With this weapon, try to aim for some Crit Rate stats to trigger the weapon passive reliably.
Kitain Cross Spear: Although the EM Main Stat isn't useful for Yun Jin, the passive decreases her Energy Recharge requirements by a lot, especially at higher refinements. It can reduce her ER% reqs depending on refinement. Note that she must cast Elemental Burst first before Skill, as the passive drains 3 energy initially when Skill is cast. This weapon can be left at level 1 while still getting full effect from the passive.

Regarding Artifact Main Stats and Substats Priority:
Crit Rate should only be considered when Yun Jin is equipping Favonius Lance to reliably proc the passive.

notes:
SHIELD SUPPORT
This kind of playstyle is very straightforward; stack as much HP as possible. Prioritize HP scaling polearms such as Black Tassel and artifact sets such as Tenacity of the Millelith (4) to capitalize on Zhongli's Shield. Most of the time, especially on a HP-focused Zhongli, casting Elemental Burst is a DPS loss due to the cast time, which is why you'd only want to cast Elemental Burst for the Crowd Control it offers, and not for damage.

Note that if paired with Xingqiu and/or Beidou, the damage reduction from orbitals from Xingqiu's Elemental Skill and Elemental Burst and/or Beidou's Elemental Burst can be utilized on Zhongli's shield, making it basically unbreakable.

Regarding Artifact Main Stats and Substats Priority:
Crit Rate should only be considered when Zhongli is equipping Favonius Lance to reliably proc the passive.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Lance: Use this weapon over Black Tassel if you value the team energy (Note that you need to build some amount of Crit Rate if you are running Favonius Lance to trigger the passive)

Regarding Artifact sets:
Archaic Petra (4): Can be more useful than Tenacity of the Millelith (4) in teams that don't scale as well with ATK buffs or already have another Tenacity of the Millelith holder. Though it can be an awkward set to utilize well without extending rotation times, since the Holder of the set (Zhongli in this case) has to pickup the shard for the buff to take effect.
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4): Preferred over Archaic Petra if there are multiple Crystallize-compatible Elements or Geo characters that want DMG% bonus.
Tenacity of the Millelith (4): This set is best in slot for Shield HP, but it's usually inconsistent against Bosses and needs good Construct placement, due to it's inconsistency, it's better to use other buffing sets that are mentioned if they work for the team.
Deepwood Memories (4): This set works best in Dendro reaction focused teams (Bloom/Burgeon/Hyperbloom/Spread) that prefer having Deepwood Memories on Zhongli so that they can run more damage focused sets such as Gilded Dreams / Flower of Paradise Lost instead for the highest overall damage gain. That said it means keeping the enemies near your pillars to have good uptime on the Dendro resistance shred effect, which can be difficult with no grouping abilities in a team.
Instructor (4): This set is usually only used in a few heavily reaction focused teams, such as Ganyu Melt or Hu Tao Vape, where you're maximising your damage in exchange for a weaker shield. Though Zhongli's shield is still usually more than strong enough even with fewer substat rolls to strengthen it.

BURST SUPPORT
Zhongli can be utilized as a Geo Burst damage dealer due to his short Elemental Burst cooldown (12s) and low energy cost (40 Energy). His Elemental Skill Hold Cast (Shield) scales with HP and at Ascension 4, you would unlock a talent that allows your Elemental Burst to scale with 33% of Zhongli's Max HP, making him a great source of Shield and damage. Though in certain teams without either very high investment into his damage or enough time in the rotation to burst without extending rotation times his burst can be a DPS loss.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Lance: If the team values the particles from this weapon, then it can be better than all the other options mentioned for overall team damage (Note that you need to build some amount of Crit Rate if you are running Favonius Lance to trigger the passive)

Regarding Artifact Main Stats:
ATK sands provides more damage than HP sands, but it does have lesser Shield HP. If you want more Shield HP, you can consider running a HP sands.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Noblesse Oblige (2) Archaic Petra (2): On lower ER requirements where Zhongli doesn't need to worry about his energy needs, this set pulls ahead of Emblem of Severed Fate (4).
Noblesse Oblige (4): This set provides Zhongli more utility when casting his Elemental Burst, you can use this set on Zhongli if there is nobody else on the team who's equipping this set.
Tenacity of the Millelith (4): Just like Noblesse Oblige (4), this set provides Zhongli with more utility if his Elemental Skill hits an enemy. This set also provides Zhongli with stronger shields.

builds.tips:
Zibai requires another Moonsign character in her team to unlock a large portion of her damage potential.

Full Combo:
E 4[N4E] - Replace 1 N4E with N1QE if burst is available
E n4 n4 E n1E n1E QE - Condensed rotation, does not offer more damage and tends to put you in teammate's skill cooldown time, only used when needing front-loaded damage
Combo for Zibai can be unreliable due to her teams generally having slower off - field Hydro appliers that interfere with her proccing Lunar-Crystallized at the right moment, which makes her combo more flexible to use depending on the situation

notes:
DPS
Zibai is an on-field damage dealer that focuses on getting enough stacks to use her enhanced Elemental Skill via using Normal Attacks and proccing Lunar-Crystallized reaction. This leads to Zibai wanting as much field time as possible to make use of all 4 of her Skill charges along with getting to the final hits of her NA chain. 

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Harbinger of Dawn [R5]: Ranking assumes the passive is active 90% of the time. Without consistent healing/shielding, this weapon performs worse than Flute of Ezpitzal [R5].
Splendor of Tranquil Waters: Ranking assumes no HP fluctuation.
Light of Foliar Incision: Assumes Zibai uses all 28 stacks.
Haran Geppaku Futsu: Assumes passive is at max stacks.
Uraku Misugiri: Assumes passive effect is doubled.
